messages.content:
What does the term DevSecOps refer to?


integrating security measures into software development and operations from the start


creating governance policies to direct development and operational compliance


using risk management analysis to ensure improved security of operations


conforming to external security requirements while improving internal operations
integrating security measures into software development and operations from the start

An international pharmaceutical company is fully compliant with local and international regulations. However, they suffered a major data breach that exploited a six-month-old vulnerability. The CIO asked Accenture "How can this be possible?"

How should Accenture respond?


Because compliance to local and international laws does not necessarily enforce security.


Because the Security Operations team does not handle compliance issues.


Because the security policy documentation was not kept up to date.


Because the local and international compliance laws conflict.
Because compliance to local and international laws does not necessarily enforce security.

What does “The Principle of Least Privilege” mean as applied to security?


Each user should have only the absolute minimum permissions necessary to complete their current responsibilities.


Permanent access to an organization’s most critical assets should be limited to the current administrator.


Access to sensitive data should be granted only to executives in the company.


Users who were recently hired within an organization should hold the lowest levels of user permissions.
Each user should have only the absolute minimum permissions necessary to complete their current responsibilities.

What is Accenture’s approach when it comes to helping our clients with security?


Create a universal security solution to fit the needs of all clients. Accenture's unique platform can be embedded in any client architecture.


Embed security in all aspects of the client work, solving even the most complex of the client’s cyber challenges.


Address the client’s security risks only after they arrive. A data breach opens all doors on selling new security deals.


Help the client create a secure, in-house data center, that follows international regulations such as ISO 27001.
Embed security in all aspects of the client work, solving even the most complex of the client’s cyber challenges.

An employee receives a phone call from someone saying they are from the bank. The person claims the purpose of the call is to verify online security information, including passwords and account numbers.

What kind of call is this?


threat intelligence


social engineering


multifactor authentication


access management
social engineering

What is the goal of Penetration (Pen) Testing?


to simulate an actual threat and track whether employees can identify it


to calculate time spent and expenditures in recovering from damage


to measure the system performance before and after migrating to the cloud


to find and exploit vulnerabilities in the environment and report them
to find and exploit vulnerabilities in the environment and report them

What is one way that Accenture can ensure that the Cloud is secure for a client?


using waterfall methodology for design improvements


blocking insecure configurations before they're made


utilizing special software to analyze all data transactions


developing accelerators to deploy custom-designed workflows
blocking insecure configurations before they're made

Which goal is Accenture trying to achieve by investing in security training for Accenture people?


empowering every person and organization


keeping ourselves and our clients secure


improving the state of the world with technology


aligning IT with business management
keeping ourselves and our clients secure

A company uses an application that controls who can connect to a sensitive database and what they can do.

What is this security feature called?


penetration testing


program and product management


identity and access management


governance and compliance
identity and access management

A large banking client recently completed migrating their entire organization to cloud. They need Accenture to help them assess how secure they are.

What benefit would the client get from involving/utilizing Accenture’s security team?


avoid only bank specific attacks such as money laundering


industry regulations and legal constraints keep Accenture from helping banks move to the Cloud


implement physical security services to protect access to the Cloud data centers


assess existing Cloud security policies and processes, protect data, and identify security improvements
assess existing Cloud security policies and processes, protect data, and identify security improvements

What is the MOST important reason for businesses to treat security as an ongoing priority?


Protecting against cyberattacks requires deep technical knowledge.


Cyber attackers are getting smarter and more sophisticated.


Internal corporate sabotage is the dominant form of attack.


Effective cyber defense requires the most cutting-edge technology.
Cyber attackers are getting smarter and more sophisticated.

An intruder with malicious intent breaks into an office and steals a hard drive containing sensitive information about the company’s business. However, when attempting to access the drive’s contents, the intruder is met with the company’s authentication protocols and data encryption measures.

What type of security is illustrated in this example?


Data Security


Product Security


Governance


Cyber Intelligence
Data Security

The Human Resources department of a large company has set up a payroll system that can only be accessed by high-level employees.

What concept is this an example of?


defense in depth


asset management


compliance


confidentiality
confidentiality

According to cyber risk managers, what role has digitization played in an organization's security?


It has compressed the size of databases, so they are easy to secure.


It has made them more vulnerable to security compromises.


The Cloud has helped them prevent security compromises.


The impact has been neutral when data is kept in the Cloud.
It has made them more vulnerable to security compromises.

A record company is using blockchain to manage the ownership of their copyrighted content. The company requires that every time one of their songs is used for commercial purposes, all the parties involved in creating it receive a set percentage of a royalty fee.

How can a blockchain help track the royalty fees?


by using consensus to determine royalties


by creating a smart contract to calculate party royalties


by granting regulators access to the blockchain


by setting specific permissions for content usage
by creating a smart contract to calculate party royalties

Accenture has put together a coalition of several ecosystem partners to implement the principles of blockchain and Multi-party Systems using varying technologies. One group of partners is referred to as MAAG.

Which companies are included in the MAAG group?


Mitsubishi, Agricultural Bank of China, Apple, Goldman Sachs


MetLife, American Express, Alphabet Inc., General Electric


Microsoft, Amazon, Alibaba, Google


Morgan Stanley, Apple, Anheuser Busch, General Motors
Microsoft, Amazon, Alibaba, Google

Accenture's Multi-party Systems practice has three areas of focus. Two of these are Financial Services and Supply Chain.

Which is the third area of focus?


Utilities


Identity


Automotive


Products
Identity
